1	package agent

4		"context"

15		agenttry "github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/agent/try"
16		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/agent/work"
17		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/bead"
18		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/config"

23	// ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime carries the non-serializable plumbing and
24	// per-invocation runtime intent for an execute-bead loop run. Durable knobs
25	// (assignee, retry caps, harness/model, tier bounds, etc.) live on
26	// config.ResolvedConfig and are passed via Run's rcfg argument.
27	type ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime struct {
28		Log             io.Writer
29		CleanupLog      io.Writer
30		CleanupRunner   executionCleanupRunner
31		CleanupInterval time.Duration
32		CleanupTickCh   <-chan time.Time
33		EventSink       io.Writer
34		ProgressCh      chan<- ProgressEvent
35		PreClaimHook    func(ctx context.Context) error
36		// PreClaimIntakeHook runs after routing preflight and before Claim. It
37		// classifies the candidate for actionability/scope; only
38		// actionable_atomic proceeds directly to Claim. Non-atomic outcomes
39		// skip the bead without claim. intake_error fail-opens so infrastructure
40		// failures do not become hidden dispatch failures.
41		PreClaimIntakeHook    PreClaimIntakeHook
42		PreDispatchLintHook   func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (LintResult, error)
43		PostAttemptTriageHook func(ctx context.Context, beadID string, report ExecuteBeadReport) (TriageResult, error)
44		// ReviewCostCap, when non-nil, accumulates reviewer cost on the same
45		// loop budget tracker used by the implementer attempts.
46		ReviewCostCap *escalation.CostCapTracker
47		// RoutePreflight, when non-nil, runs once during Run bootstrap before
48		// the drain loop starts. It is expected to call upstream ResolveRoute
49		// against the loop's resolved (harness, model) and return whatever typed
50		// routing error the upstream surfaced — notably
51		// agent.ErrHarnessModelIncompatible when the harness allow-list rejects
52		// the model. Any non-nil error stops the worker before any bead is
53		// claimed; DDx does NOT duplicate the upstream allow-list.
54		RoutePreflight func(ctx context.Context, harness, model string) error
55		// BudgetStop, when non-nil, is checked before selecting the next bead. It
56		// lets CLI/server callers surface already-tripped budget state as a typed
57		// work-layer StopCondition before any new bead is claimed.
58		BudgetStop   func() (ExecuteBeadReport, bool)
59		Once         bool
60		PollInterval time.Duration
61		NoReview     bool
62		LabelFilter  string
63		SessionID    string
64		WorkerID     string
65		ProjectRoot  string
66		// TargetBeadID, when non-empty, restricts nextCandidate to only return the
67		// named bead from the execution-ready queue. Used by `ddx try <bead-id>`
68		// to dispatch a single specific bead through the same claim → executor →
69		// land path the queue drain uses. When empty, the picker behaves normally.
70		TargetBeadID string
71		// WakeCh, when non-nil, signals the idle-poll sleep to return immediately
72		// so the loop re-scans the queue. Used by the operator-prompt approve /
73		// auto-approve mutations (Story 15) to avoid a poll-interval-sized delay
74		// before a freshly-approved bead is claimed. Implementations must send
75		// non-blocking (server-side helpers do); the loop only waits for a
76		// receive on WakeCh during the idle sleep, never elsewhere.
77		WakeCh <-chan struct{}
78	}
79	
80	// DefaultReviewMaxRetries is the number of reviewer attempts allowed per
81	// committed result_rev before the loop emits a terminal
82	// `review-manual-required` event and stops re-executing primary. FEAT-022 §14.
83	const DefaultReviewMaxRetries = 3
84	
85	// MaxLoopCooldown is the absolute upper bound the execute-loop will set for
86	// any execute-loop-retry-after value. Year-scale parks effectively mean
87	// "never retry" and that should be a deliberate operator choice via
88	// `ddx bead update --set execute-loop-retry-after=...`, not an automatic
89	// loop decision. Beyond this cap, the loop refuses to lengthen the cooldown
90	// further; an operator extending it manually still works.
91	const MaxLoopCooldown = 24 * time.Hour
92	
93	// LandConflictCooldown is the short cooldown applied when all conflict-recovery
94	// paths fail (3-way ort auto-merge + optional focused-resolve agent) and the
95	// bead is parked as land_conflict_unresolvable. Much shorter than
96	// MaxLoopCooldown because land conflicts often resolve quickly as sibling beads
97	// advance the base branch.
98	const LandConflictCooldown = 15 * time.Minute
99	
100	// StoreErrorCooldown is the short cooldown applied when a Store.* operation in
101	// the outcome-handling block returns a transient error. It prevents an
102	// immediate re-queue of the affected bead while allowing the loop to continue
103	// to the next candidate. Store errors during outcome handling are non-fatal:
104	// they are logged and emitted as loop-error events but do not terminate the
105	// worker.
106	const StoreErrorCooldown = 5 * time.Minute
107	
108	// ProviderUnavailableCooldown is the retry pause for route/provider outages
109	// where no configured tier can currently run the bead. This is transient
110	// scheduler state, not bead quality evidence, so it must not become a durable
111	// triage:needs-investigation park.
112	const ProviderUnavailableCooldown = 15 * time.Minute
113	
114	// SubmitWithPreMergeChecks is the canonical land-back step for the execute-bead
115	// loop. It runs the project's .ddx/checks/*.yaml gate against the worker's
116	// (baseRev, resultRev) before forwarding to submit. On Blocked, it preserves
117	// the worker's result under refs/ddx/iterations/, records checks-blocked
118	// events on the bead, and returns the synthesised LandResult{Status:"preserved"}
119	// without ever calling submit. On pass, honoured checks-bypass annotations are
120	// recorded as checks-bypass events and submit runs normally.
121	//
122	// submit is the project's land coordinator entry point (typically
123	// LocalLandCoordinator.Submit or the server-side coordinator submit).
124	//
125	// The implementation lives in checks_premerge.go alongside its supporting
126	// helpers (RunPreMergeChecks, AppendPreMergeChecksEvents,
127	// PreserveAfterPreMergeChecks); this thin wrapper exists so the execute-bead
128	// loop's land-back call site is one obvious helper rather than three nested
129	// ones, and so callers wiring the gate do not need to import the helper layer
130	// directly.
131	func SubmitWithPreMergeChecks(
132		ctx context.Context,
133		projectRoot string,
134		b *bead.Bead,
135		res *ExecuteBeadResult,
136		submit func(LandRequest) (*LandResult, error),
137		eventStore BeadEventAppender,
138		actor, source string,
139		now func() time.Time,
140	) (*LandResult, *PreMergeChecksOutcome, error) {
141		if now == nil {
142			now = time.Now
143		}
144		if submit == nil {
145			return nil, nil, fmt.Errorf("submit-with-pre-merge-checks: submit callback required")
146		}
147		evidenceDir := filepath.Join(projectRoot, res.ExecutionDir)
148		outcome, err := RunPreMergeChecks(ctx, projectRoot, b, res.BaseRev, res.ResultRev, evidenceDir)
149		if err != nil {
150			// Treat checks_bypass / loader errors as a hard preserve: the worker
151			// did its job; the operator misconfigured the gate. Better to park
152			// the result under an iteration ref than to silently land it.
153			ref := PreserveRef(res.BeadID, res.BaseRev)
154			if upErr := (&RealOrchestratorGitOps{}).UpdateRef(projectRoot, ref, res.ResultRev); upErr != nil {
155				return nil, nil, fmt.Errorf("preserving result after checks setup error: %w (original error: %v)", upErr, err)
156			}
157			return &LandResult{
158				Status:      "preserved",
159				PreserveRef: ref,
160				Reason:      "pre-merge checks setup error: " + err.Error(),
161			}, nil, nil
162		}
163		if outcome.Blocked {
164			land, perr := PreserveAfterPreMergeChecks(projectRoot, res, outcome, &RealOrchestratorGitOps{})
165			if perr != nil {
166				return nil, outcome, perr
167			}
168			if eventStore != nil {
169				_ = AppendPreMergeChecksEvents(eventStore, res.BeadID, outcome, actor, source, now().UTC())
170			}
171			return land, outcome, nil
172		}
173		if eventStore != nil && len(outcome.Bypassed) > 0 {
174			_ = AppendPreMergeChecksEvents(eventStore, res.BeadID, outcome, actor, source, now().UTC())
175		}
176		land, sErr := submit(BuildLandRequestFromResult(projectRoot, res))
177		return land, outcome, sErr
178	}
179	
180	// runPostAttemptTriage invokes the optional post-attempt triage hook after
181	// the attempt outcome has been finalized but before any cooldown decision is
182	// applied. Hook failures are fail-open: the report is returned unchanged and
183	// legacy cooldown behavior remains in force.
184	func (w *ExecuteBeadWorker) runPostAttemptTriage(ctx context.Context, candidate bead.Bead, report ExecuteBeadReport, runtime ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime, assignee string, now func() time.Time) ExecuteBeadReport {
185		hook := runtime.PostAttemptTriageHook
186		if hook == nil {
187			return report
188		}
189		triage, err := hook(ctx, candidate.ID, report)
190		if err != nil {
191			if runtime.Log != nil {
192				_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(runtime.Log, "post-attempt triage error (%s %s): %v (continuing)\n", candidate.ID, report.Status, err)
193			}
194			return report
195		}
196		triage = normalizeTriageResult(triage)
197		hasWarnings := len(triage.DecodeWarnings) > 0 || triage.Malformed
198		classificationRecognized := isRecognizedTriageClassification(triage.Classification)
199		if hasWarnings && runtime.Log != nil {
200			_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(runtime.Log, "post-attempt triage warning (%s %s): %s (continuing)\n", candidate.ID, report.Status, formatTriageWarnings(triage.DecodeWarnings))

200			_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(runtime.Log, "post-attempt triage warning (%s %s): %s (continuing)\n", candidate.ID, report.Status, formatTriageWarnings(triage.DecodeWarnings))
201		}
202		if !classificationRecognized && !hasWarnings {
203			return report
204		}
205		if classificationRecognized {
206			report.OutcomeReason = triage.Classification
207		}
208		recordPostAttemptTriageEvent(w.Store, candidate.ID, report, triage, assignee, now().UTC())
209		return report
210	}
211	
212	// CapLoopCooldown clamps a loop-set cooldown duration to MaxLoopCooldown.
213	// The loop uses this for every SetExecutionCooldown call so no automatic
214	// path can silently park a bead for a year.
215	func CapLoopCooldown(d time.Duration) time.Duration {
216		if d <= 0 {
217			return 0
218		}
219		if d > MaxLoopCooldown {
220			return MaxLoopCooldown
221		}
222		return d
223	}
224	
225	type ExecuteBeadReport struct {
226		BeadID    string `json:"bead_id"`
227		AttemptID string `json:"attempt_id,omitempty"`
228		WorkerID  string `json:"worker_id,omitempty"`
229		Harness   string `json:"harness,omitempty"`
230		Provider  string `json:"provider,omitempty"`
231		Model     string `json:"model,omitempty"`
232		// ActualPower is the routing-decision power of the model that actually
233		// served the implementer's call. Forwarded to the post-merge reviewer so
234		// it can request MinPower=actualPower+1 and bias routing toward a
235		// stronger reviewer (R4 pairing).
236		ActualPower                 int           `json:"actual_power,omitempty"`
237		PredictedPower              int           `json:"predicted_power,omitempty"`
238		PredictedSpeedTPS           float64       `json:"predicted_speed_tps,omitempty"`
239		PredictedCostUSDPer1kTokens float64       `json:"predicted_cost_usd_per_1k_tokens,omitempty"`
240		PredictedCostSource         string        `json:"predicted_cost_source,omitempty"`
241		Status                      string        `json:"status"`
242		Detail                      string        `json:"detail,omitempty"`
243		Error                       string        `json:"error,omitempty"`
244		Stderr                      string        `json:"stderr,omitempty"`
245		RateLimitBudget             time.Duration `json:"rate_limit_budget,omitempty"`
246		SessionID                   string        `json:"session_id,omitempty"`
247		BaseRev                     string        `json:"base_rev,omitempty"`
248		ResultRev                   string        `json:"result_rev,omitempty"`
249		// ImplementationRev is the worker's own commit SHA before landing.
250		// Mirrors ExecuteBeadResult.ImplementationRev; empty for no-changes attempts.
251		ImplementationRev string `json:"implementation_rev,omitempty"`
252		// LandedRev is the target branch tip after the coordinator landing.
253		// Distinct from ImplementationRev on the merge-commit path.
254		LandedRev string `json:"landed_rev,omitempty"`
255		// EvidenceRev is the trailing evidence commit SHA when distinct from
256		// ImplementationRev. Empty when not separately committed.
257		EvidenceRev string `json:"evidence_rev,omitempty"`
258		PreserveRef string `json:"preserve_ref,omitempty"`
259		// CandidateRef is the project-root git ref pinned before checks and review.
260		// Format: refs/ddx/iterations/<attempt-id>/<cycle-index>.
261		CandidateRef string `json:"candidate_ref,omitempty"`
262		// CycleIndex is the zero-based repair-cycle index for this candidate.
263		CycleIndex int    `json:"cycle_index,omitempty"`
264		RetryAfter string `json:"retry_after,omitempty"`
265		// NoChangesRationale carries the agent's explanation when status == no_changes.
266		NoChangesRationale string `json:"no_changes_rationale,omitempty"`
267		// ReviewVerdict is the post-merge review verdict (APPROVE, REQUEST_CHANGES,
268		// or BLOCK) when a reviewer ran. Empty when review was skipped.
269		ReviewVerdict string `json:"review_verdict,omitempty"`
270		// ReviewRationale carries the actionable reviewer-authored findings for
271		// non-APPROVE review outcomes.
272		ReviewRationale string `json:"review_rationale,omitempty"`
273		// Tier is the model tier used for the final attempt (cheap, standard, smart).
274		// Populated by tier-escalating executors; empty for single-tier attempts.
275		Tier string `json:"tier,omitempty"`
276		// ProbeResult is a brief summary of the provider health probe at attempt time.
277		ProbeResult string `json:"probe_result,omitempty"`
278		// CostUSD is the dollar cost of this attempt as reported by the harness.
279		// Tier-escalating executors propagate this so the escalation trace can
280		// compute wasted/effective spend.
281		CostUSD float64 `json:"cost_usd,omitempty"`
282		// DurationMS is the wall-clock duration of this attempt.
283		DurationMS int64 `json:"duration_ms,omitempty"`
284		// Profile routing telemetry. Populated when execute-loop uses a profile
285		// ladder rather than an explicit harness/model pin.
286		RequestedProfile string `json:"requested_profile,omitempty"`
287		RequestedTier    string `json:"requested_tier,omitempty"`
288		ResolvedTier     string `json:"resolved_tier,omitempty"`
289		EscalationCount  int    `json:"escalation_count,omitempty"`
290		FinalTier        string `json:"final_tier,omitempty"`
291		// DecompositionRecommendation carries the structured list of recommended
292		// sub-bead titles when Status == declined_needs_decomposition. The loop
293		// records these on the bead as a `decomposition-recommendation` event so
294		// operators (or helix-evolve) can split the bead without re-deriving the
295		// recommendation.
296		DecompositionRecommendation []string `json:"decomposition_recommendation,omitempty"`
297		// DecompositionRationale is a free-form explanation accompanying
298		// DecompositionRecommendation. Optional.
299		DecompositionRationale string `json:"decomposition_rationale,omitempty"`
300		// Disrupted marks a failed attempt as worker-disrupted rather than
301		// model-gave-up: the model was prevented from making progress by an
302		// external cause (context cancellation, executor SIGKILL/SIGTERM,
303		// transport-class provider error, server restart, routing preflight
304		// rejection). The execute-loop bypasses the no-progress cooldown for
305		// Disrupted reports so the bead is immediately re-claimable instead of
306		// being parked for hours on a transient outage. ddx-5b3e57f4.
307		Disrupted bool `json:"disrupted,omitempty"`
308		// DisruptionReason carries the kind of disruption detected when
309		// Disrupted=true (e.g. "context_canceled", "context_deadline",
310		// "transport_error", "preflight_rejected"). Used in the
311		// `disruption_detected` event body so operators can see which class is
312		// occurring.
313		DisruptionReason string `json:"disruption_reason,omitempty"`
314		// OutcomeReason carries the machine-readable lifecycle classification for
315		// the attempt outcome. It complements Disrupted/DisruptionReason without
316		// changing their mechanical interruption semantics.
317		OutcomeReason string `json:"outcome_reason,omitempty"`
318		// ResourceExhausted carries the execution-root preflight result when the
319		// attempt stopped before the agent could safely continue.
320		ResourceExhausted any `json:"resource_exhausted,omitempty"`
321	}
322	
323	type ExecuteBeadExecutor interface {
324		Execute(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (ExecuteBeadReport, error)
325	}
326	
327	type ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (ExecuteBeadReport, error)
328	
329	func (f ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc) Execute(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
330		return f(ctx, beadID)
331	}
332	
333	type ExecuteBeadLoopStore interface {
334		ReadyExecution() ([]bead.Bead, error)
335		Get(id string) (*bead.Bead, error)
336		Claim(id, assignee string) error
337		Unclaim(id string) error
338		TouchClaimHeartbeat(id string) error
339		CloseWithEvidence(id, sessionID, commitSHA string) error
340		AppendEvent(id string, event bead.BeadEvent) error
341		Events(id string) ([]bead.BeadEvent, error)
342		SetExecutionCooldown(id string, until time.Time, status, detail string) error
343		AppendNotes(id string, notes string) error
344		IncrNoChangesCount(id string) (int, error)
345		// Reopen sets a closed bead back to open, appending notes to the bead's
346		// Notes field and recording a reopen event. Used by review workflows that
347		// intentionally transition a bead back into open state.
348		Reopen(id, reason, notes string) error
349		// Update mutates a bead in place. Used by the post-merge triage step to
350		// add labels (e.g. "needs_human") and metadata hints (e.g. tier-pin) when
351		// the triage policy escalates after repeated review BLOCKs.
352		Update(id string, mutate func(*bead.Bead)) error
353	}
354	
355	// readyDiagnoser is the optional interface the work loop uses to explain an
356	// empty execution queue. bead.Store satisfies it via ReadyExecutionBreakdown.
357	type readyDiagnoser interface {
358		ReadyExecutionBreakdown() (bead.ReadyExecutionBreakdown, error)
359	}
360	
361	// NoReadyWorkBreakdown explains why the execution-ready queue is empty when
362	// dependency-ready beads exist. Populated on an ExecuteBeadLoopResult when
363	// NoReadyWork fires and the store exposes ReadyExecutionBreakdown.
364	type NoReadyWorkBreakdown struct {
365		SkippedEpics                 []string `json:"skipped_epics,omitempty"`
366		SkippedEpicClosureCandidates []string `json:"skipped_epic_closure_candidates,omitempty"`
367		SkippedOnCooldown            []string `json:"skipped_on_cooldown,omitempty"`
368		SkippedNeedsInvestigation    []string `json:"skipped_needs_investigation,omitempty"`
369		SkippedBlocked               []string `json:"skipped_blocked,omitempty"`
370		SkippedNotEligible           []string `json:"skipped_not_eligible,omitempty"`
371		SkippedSuperseded            []string `json:"skipped_superseded,omitempty"`
372		NextRetryAfter               string   `json:"next_retry_after,omitempty"`
373	}
374	
375	// ProgressEvent is the FEAT-006 structured progress event. It is defined
376	// separately in the server package (server.ProgressEvent); this alias lets
377	// the agent package emit events without importing the server package.
378	// The field names and types are identical — the server package deserialises
379	// these directly from the channel.
380	//
381	// Terminal phases: done, preserved, failed.
382	type ProgressEvent struct {
383		EventID   string    `json:"event_id"`
384		AttemptID string    `json:"attempt_id"`
385		WorkerID  string    `json:"worker_id"`
386		ProjectID string    `json:"project_id"`
387		BeadID    string    `json:"bead_id"`
388		Harness   string    `json:"harness,omitempty"`
389		Model     string    `json:"model,omitempty"`
390		Profile   string    `json:"profile,omitempty"`
391		Phase     string    `json:"phase"`
392		PhaseSeq  int       `json:"phase_seq"`
393		Heartbeat bool      `json:"heartbeat"`
394		TS        time.Time `json:"ts"`
395		ElapsedMS int64     `json:"elapsed_ms"`
396		Message   string    `json:"message,omitempty"`
397	}
398	
399	type ExecuteBeadLoopResult struct {
